questions.question: What does the interquartile range (IQR) represent in a box-and-whisker plot?

options.A: The range of the data set
options.B: The median of the data set
options.C: The difference between Q3 and Q1
options.D: The range of the middle 50% of the data
options.E: The standard deviation of the data

questions.question: A box-and-whisker plot shows a dataset with $Q_1 = 12$, $Q_3 = 20$, and a median of $16$. What is the interquartile range (IQR)?
Which of the following statements about box-and-whisker plots is FALSE?

options.A: The median is always at the center of the box
options.B: Outliers are always below $Q_1$ or above $Q_3$
options.C: The interquartile range (IQR) is always positive
options.D: A box-and-whisker plot can be used to compare multiple datasets
options.E: The box represents the range of the data

questions.question: In a study on the dosage of a new medicine, the box-and-whisker plot shows an outlier at $25mg$. If $Q_1 = 10mg$ and $Q_3 = 18mg$, what can be concluded about the outlier?

options.A: It is a valid data point
options.B: It is an error in data collection
options.C: It is more than $1.5 \times IQR$ away from $Q_3$
options.D: It is less than $1.5 \times IQR$ away from $Q_3$
options.E: It is the median of the dataset

questions.question: A dataset of cell growth rates has a box-and-whisker plot with $Q_1 = 2 \frac{mm}{day}$, $Q_3 = 4 \frac{mm}{day}$, and a median of $3 \frac{mm}{day}$. What is the interquartile range (IQR)?
Which of the following box-and-whisker plots represents a dataset with the largest interquartile range (IQR)?
A box-and-whisker plot shows the distribution of medicine dosages with $Q_1 = 5mg$, $Q_3 = 15mg$, and an outlier at $30mg$. What can be concluded about the outlier?

options.B: It is more than $1.5 \times IQR$ away from $Q_3$
options.C: It is the median of the dataset

questions.question: Which of the following statements about outliers in a box-and-whisker plot is TRUE?

options.C: Outliers can be below $Q_1$ or above $Q_3$
options.D: Outliers are never below $Q_1$ or above $Q_3$
options.E: Outliers are the median of the dataset

questions.question: Construct a box-and-whisker plot for the dataset: $12, 15, 18, 20, 22, 25, 30, 35, 40$. Identify the quartiles, interquartile range (IQR), and any outliers.

A study on cell growth rates collected the following data: $2 \frac{mm}{day}$, $2.5 \frac{mm}{day}$, $3 \frac{mm}{day}$, $3.5 \frac{mm}{day}$, $4 \frac{mm}{day}$, $4.5 \frac{mm}{day}$, $10 \frac{mm}{day}$. Create a box-and-whisker plot and determine if there are any outliers. Justify your answer.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Ensure students understand the concept of interquartile range (IQR) and how to calculate it
Tip 2: Emphasize the importance of identifying outliers in a dataset
Tip 3: Remind students to carefully examine the box-and-whisker plot to determine the quartiles and IQR
